methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rhoman Aerospace is a seed-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by Thomas Youmans and Thomas Callen. The valuation universe consists of seven private peers in the sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Thoron. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. While four same-sector peers have been identified in Drones & Autonomous, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category—below the threshold required for meaningful statistical analysis. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable methodological distortion given the heterogeneity of business models and maturity profiles within Drones & Autonomous.

Given the data constraints, a defensible valuation range cannot be triangulated at this time. The analysis is constrained by an insufficient number of seed-stage or near-peer comparables with disclosed valuation metrics. To support a valuation conclusion, additional comparable transaction data or disclosed funding round values from narrower peer sets would be required. Key uncertainties include the absence of public market benchmarks and the stage-specific illiquidity discount applicable to seed-stage frontier-tech investments.
